subsection.heading: Stock and Dividends.—
subsection.content: The corporation may not issue stock or declare or pay a dividend.

subsection.heading: Political Activities.—
subsection.content: The corporation, or a director or officer of the corporation as such, may not contribute to, support, or participate in any political activity or in any manner attempt to influence legislation.

subsection.heading: Loan.—
subsection.content: The corporation may not make a loan to a director, officer, or employee of the corporation.

subsection.heading: Claim of Governmental Approval or Authority.—
subsection.content: The corporation may not claim congressional approval, or the authority of the United States, for any activity of the corporation.

subsection.heading: Corporate Status.—
subsection.content: The corporation shall maintain its status as a corporation incorporated under the laws of the State of New York.
